MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR OCT-15-2018 - FRANCIS MCKEOWN, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF GREGORY NOTO PRESENT, NO APPEARANCE AT TRIAL CALL BY HIS COUNSEL, AMY ANDERSON. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL RELAYED TO THE COURT THAT COMMUNICATION WAS JUST RECEIVED THIS MORNING FROM PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL THAT SHE IS UNABLE TO REPRESENT PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF REQUESTED AN EXTENSION OF TIME TO OBTAIN NEW COUNSEL & DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL INDICATED HE WOULD NOT OPPOSE ANY CONTINUANCE. THE COURT FOUND GOOD CAUSE TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE. PLAINTIFF IS IN THE PROCESS OF OBTAINING NEW COUNSEL. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO OCT-23-2018 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206 TO SET A NEW TRIAL DATE. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; MATTER NOT REPORTED (206/TLJ)

MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR DEC-11-2018 CONTINUED TO MASTER CALENDAR COURT ON JUN-03-2019 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF GREGORY NOTO PRESENT WITHOUT HIS ATTORNEY OF RECORD, AMY ANDERSON. LIMITED SCOPE COUNSEL JONATHAN NIGHTINGALE APPEARED FOR PLAINTIFF. FRANCIS MCKEOWN, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. THE COURT SANCTIONS AMY ANDERSON, ESQ. IN THE AMOUNT OF $500.00 FOR FAILURE TO APPEAR OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO SUBSTITUTE OUT OF THE CASE. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS AMY ANDERSON, ESQ. IS ORDERED TO SHOW CAUSE ON DEC-18-2018 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. FAILURE OF AMY ANDERSON, ESQ. TO APPEAR AT THIS HEARING MAY RESULT IN FURTHER SANCTIONS AND A REPORT TO THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA. ALL OTHER PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO APPEAR ON 12/18/18 AND WAIVED FURTHER NOTICE. THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 6/3/19 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL DATES TO TRACK THE NEW TRIAL DATE. THE COURT GRANTS AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME FOR A MOTION TO REMOVE AMY ANDERSON AS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR PLAINTIFF AND SUBSTITUTE IN WITH A HEARING DATE OF 12/18/18 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS ARE WAIVED. MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO BRING AT THE NEXT HEARING DATE. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GREGORY NOTO's Motion For Order Vacating And Setting Aside Judgment Of Dismissal and Return Case to Active Civil Status - Plaintiff Gregory Noto's motion for order vacating dismissal filed January 10, 2017 is granted. The declaration of Ms. Anderson establishes Mr. Noto's entitlement to the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Per those provisions, Ms. Anderson is directed to pay the reasonable compensatory legal costs to counsel for defendant Jason Perkins or Mr. Perkins. Order signed in open court.
